source_jobs.title: Senior Piping Engineer (Power Generation / Gas-to-Power)
source_jobs.description:
What We’re Building

Candid is building the AI layer for EPCs.

We use AI to reduce the manual effort behind preconstruction engineering by automating document understanding, consistency checks, and repetitive workflow steps across major disciplines. Our goal is to make preconstruction 10x faster without sacrificing engineering quality.

We recently raised $6.5M from top AI and industrial investors (including Schneider Electric and Meta’s Chief AI Scientist). Our team includes engineers from MIT, Carnegie Mellon, major LNG and power projects, and leading EPCs.

This Role

We're hiring a senior piping engineer who has actually delivered power generation and gas-to-power projects at a top EPC, PMC, or owner-operator. Think combined cycle, simple cycle and peakers, gas-to-power for data centers and behind-the-meter campuses, LNG, and cogeneration, with the high-energy steam cycle and balance of plant piping that goes with them. If your career has been refining or petrochem process piping with no power work, this isn't the right fit.

This is a hands-on role. You'll do real piping engineering on our delivery work for the parts our software can't handle yet, you'll be the quality check on what the software does produce, and you'll shape where the product goes next. Three jobs in one, and the mix shifts as the product gets better.



What You'll Do

This is hands-on engineering plus product. Day to day you'll:

 - Do real piping engineering on our FEL-2 and early-phase delivery work, owning the parts our software can't handle yet so projects ship

 - Be the quality gate on Candid's automated piping outputs: catch what's wrong before it reaches a client, and feed it back so the product gets sharper

 - Define how piping information gets developed and validated through FEED and early detailed design on power and gas-to-power projects, and what "good" looks like

 - Review and validate piping content across P&IDs, line lists, piping specs, and tie-in lists

 - Build repeatable checks that catch tagging, completeness, and consistency issues early

 - Map where piping rework happens, why it happens, and what inputs drive it

 - Clarify interface points with Process, Mechanical, I&C, Electrical, and Civil/Structural (pipe racks and supports)

 - Separate the steps that are genuinely repetitive from the ones that need real engineering judgment

You'll help define the checks, workflows, and product requirements that let software take on the repetitive parts, with engineers in control.



What We're Looking For

 - 7+ years at a large scale EPC, PMC, or owner-operator, with most of it on power generation or gas-to-power

 - Direct experience on combined cycle, simple cycle, gas-to-power, LNG, or cogeneration projects (not refining or petrochem only)

 - You've personally owned line list and piping spec development through FEED

 - Strong on power plant piping: high-energy steam cycle (main steam, hot and cold reheat, feedwater), plus fuel gas, condensate, cooling water, and balance of plant piping

 - Hands-on with ASME B31.1 (power piping) and B31.3 (process piping)

 - Familiarity with pipe stress analysis and stress-critical line identification (CAESAR II or similar)

 - Experience with plot plan and 3D model driven layout (AVEVA E3D / PDMS, SmartPlant 3D, or similar)

 - You spot inconsistencies across P&IDs, line lists, and vendor packages fast

 - Clear understanding of cross-discipline dependencies

 - You've felt boxed in by big-company process and you want out

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and wearing multiple hats; bias to action over waiting for perfect



Nice to Have

 - Discipline lead or technical authority experience

 - Work on data center or behind-the-meter power campuses

 - High-energy piping materials and metallurgy (P91, P22 chrome-moly), or pipe support and spring hanger design

 - Brownfield tie-ins and constructability constraints

 - Real interest in AI and automation



Who Thrives Here

You've outgrown the big-f

source_jobs.title: Senior Electrical Engineer (Power Generation / Gas-to-Power)
source_jobs.description:
What We’re Building

Candid is building the AI layer for EPCs.

We use AI to reduce the manual effort behind preconstruction engineering by automating document understanding, consistency checks, and repetitive workflow steps across major disciplines. Our goal is to make preconstruction 10x faster without sacrificing engineering quality.

We recently raised $6.5M from top AI and industrial investors (including Schneider Electric and Meta’s Chief AI Scientist). Our team includes engineers from MIT, Carnegie Mellon, major LNG and power projects, and leading EPCs.

This Role

We're hiring a senior electrical engineer who has actually delivered power generation and gas-to-power projects at a top EPC, PMC, owner-operator, or utility. Think combined cycle, simple cycle and peakers, gas-to-power for data centers and behind-the-meter campuses, LNG, and cogeneration, including the switchyard and grid interconnection. If your career has been refining or petrochem electrical with no power work, this isn't the right fit.

This is a hands-on role. You'll do real electrical engineering on our delivery work for the parts our software can't handle yet, you'll be the quality check on what the software does produce, and you'll shape where the product goes next. Three jobs in one, and the mix shifts as the product gets better.



What You'll Do

This is hands-on engineering plus product. Day to day you'll:

 - Do real electrical engineering on our FEL-2 and early-phase delivery work, owning the parts our software can't handle yet so projects ship

 - Be the quality gate on Candid's automated electrical outputs: catch what's wrong before it reaches a client, and feed it back so the product gets sharper

 - Define how electrical scope gets developed and validated through FEED on power and gas-to-power projects, and what "good" looks like

 - Review and validate electrical deliverables across early-phase packages: single-lines, load lists, transformer and switchgear sizing, distribution architecture, grounding

 - Build repeatable checks that catch inconsistencies across load assumptions, equipment requirements, and package interfaces

 - Map where electrical rework happens, why it happens, and what inputs drive it

 - Clarify interface points with Mechanical, Process, Instrumentation, and the grid interconnection

 - Separate the steps that are genuinely repetitive from the ones that need real engineering judgment

You'll help define the checks, workflows, and product requirements that let software take on the repetitive parts, with engineers in control.



What We're Looking For

 - 7+ years at a large scale EPC, PMC, owner-operator, or utility, with most of it on power generation or gas-to-power

 - Direct experience on combined cycle, simple cycle, gas-to-power, LNG, or cogeneration projects (not refining or petrochem only)

 - You've personally owned single-line diagram and electrical load list development through FEED

 - Strong on power system architecture: GSU and station service transformers, MV/LV switchgear, MCCs, distribution, grounding

 - Hands-on with power system study tools: ETAP, SKM PowerTools, EasyPower, PSS/E, or similar (load flow, short circuit, coordination, arc flash)

 - Working knowledge of NEC/NFPA 70, IEEE, NESC, NERC, and IEC

 - Familiarity with grid interconnection and the switchyard interface

 - You spot inconsistencies across load lists, single-lines, and vendor packages fast

 - Clear understanding of cross-discipline dependencies

 - You've felt boxed in by big-company process and you want out

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and wearing multiple hats; bias to action over waiting for perfect



Nice to Have

 - Discipline lead or technical authority experience

 - Work on data center or behind-the-meter power campuses

 - Brownfield tie-ins and constrained or islanded power systems

 - Protection and relaying philosophy, or interconnection study experience

 - Real interest in AI and automation



Who Thrives Here

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer (EPC and Owner/Operator) - Houston
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE’RE BUILDING

Candid is building the AI layer for EPCs and owner operators.

We use AI to reduce the manual effort behind preconstruction engineering by automating document understanding, consistency checks, and repetitive workflow steps across major disciplines. The goal is to make preconstruction 10x faster without sacrificing engineering quality.

We recently raised $6.5M from top AI and industrial investors (including Schneider Electric and Meta’s Chief AI Scientist). Our team includes engineers from MIT, Carnegie Mellon, major LNG and power projects, and leading EPCs.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

You are the tip of the spear in Houston. Your job is to get us into the right rooms and close deals.

You will own early revenue in the EPC and owner operator space, with a strong bias toward smaller, faster moving firms.

Core responsibilities

 - Build and own a Houston based pipeline of EPCs, PMCs, and owner operators

 - Drive new meetings weekly through outbound, warm intros, events, and local networking

 - Run discovery calls and qualify deals fast (use case, urgency, buying process, champion, budget)

 - Deliver product demos that feel credible to engineers and leadership

 - Translate real workflows into a clear value story and ROI that customers actually believe

 - Scope pilots and proofs of value with clear success criteria, deliverables, timeline, and next step to production

 - Coordinate technical deep dives with our team when needed and keep momentum high

 - Handle common objections (security, data access, accuracy, “we already have a process,” “AI risk”)

 - Support RFPs, security questionnaires, procurement steps, and contracting workflows

 - Keep CRM clean and run a tight sales process from first meeting to signature

You will work closely with the founders and product team. You will shape our positioning and playbook in the market because you will hear the real objections and real motivations first.




WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years selling into EPCs, industrial services firms, or owner operators (engineering software or technical solutions)

 - Deep understanding of how EPC preconstruction works (FEL, FEED, early detailed design) and how decisions get made

 - You can speak engineer and exec and you know when to go deep vs when to zoom out

 - Strong Houston network and the ability to keep building it (events, lunches, coffees, conferences)

 - Proven ability to source meetings and close deals, not just “manage accounts”

 - Comfort selling something new where the category is still forming

 - Strong writing and follow up skills, you keep momentum without being annoying

 - High pace. You like being on the ground, in person, and moving fast




PREFERENCE

 - Experience working with smaller EPCs or regional owner operators and building relationships there

 - You are biased toward companies that move fast and don’t take 12 months to buy




NICE TO HAVE

 - Prior role as a sales engineer, solutions engineer, or technical BD in industrial software

 - Experience selling workflows related to engineering documents (PFDs, P&IDs, datasheets, vendor packages, line lists, specs)

 - Familiarity with typical enterprise procurement and security reviews

 - Existing relationships with project directors, discipline leads, engineering managers, or digital teams in Houston




COMPENSATION AND PERKS

 - Base + meaningful equity + commission

 - Commission tied directly to closed revenue (simple and transparent)

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Insurance with medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - Health and wellness budget up to $150 per month

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering Delivery (Power Generation / Gas-to-Power)
source_jobs.description:
What We're Building

Candid is building the AI layer for EPCs and owner operators.

We use AI to reduce the manual effort behind preconstruction engineering by automating document understanding, consistency checks, and repetitive workflow steps across major disciplines. The goal is to make preconstruction 10x faster without sacrificing engineering quality.

We recently raised $6.5M from top AI and industrial investors (including Schneider Electric and Meta’s Chief AI Scientist). Our team includes engineers from MIT, Carnegie Mellon, major LNG and power projects, and leading EPCs.



Why This Role Exists

We need someone who has lived inside Pre-FEED and FEED. Someone who knows what a design basis package actually looks like at 3 AM before a gate review, who has directed engineers through datasheet cycles, and who has felt the pain of building the same thing from scratch on every project.

You are not here to manage a backlog. You are here to direct real project delivery for Candid's clients and run the engineering team doing it, while shaping the AI tools that redefine how this work gets done. You are the bridge between deep domain knowledge and a product team building at startup speed.



This Role

This is a player-coach role at a startup, not a director seat at a 5,000-person EPC. You'll have a small, sharp discipline team reporting to you, not three layers and an army. You'll direct delivery and manage the team, and you'll also personally own quality and roll up your sleeves on the work when it counts, especially early. If the last time you touched a deliverable was a decade ago and you only direct through others, this isn't the right fit.

Day to day you'll:

 - Direct Pre-FEED and FEED engagements for Candid's clients across power generation, gas-to-power, LNG, midstream, and downstream projects

 - Lead and manage the discipline engineering team (process, electrical, mechanical, piping), set the bar, and own delivery quality end to end

 - Own the assembly and quality of design basis packages, heat and mass balances, single-lines, equipment lists, P&IDs and PFDs, datasheets, utility summaries, and gate-review packages

 - Be the authoritative engineering voice in client conversations with project development managers, VPs of Projects, and heads of engineering

 - Work directly with our product and AI team to turn real Pre-FEED and FEED workflows into automation targets. What takes 600 hours today should take 60 tomorrow

 - Be the quality gate on Candid's automated outputs: catch what's wrong before it reaches a client, and feed it back so the product gets sharper

 - Advise on execution strategy, PDRI scoring, and gate-review readiness for client engagements

 - Shape Candid's delivery methodology and standards as we scale from early clients to a repeatable practice



Who You Are

 - 15+ years in capital project execution, with significant time in power generation, gas-to-power, LNG, midstream, or petrochemical facilities

 - You have directed or led Pre-FEED, FEED, or Concept Select phases end to end. Directed, not just participated

 - Direct experience leading power or gas-to-power projects (combined cycle, simple cycle, cogeneration, or gas-to-power for data center and BTM campuses). Multi-sector experience across LNG, midstream, and downstream on top of that is a strong plus, not a requirement to explain away

 - You've managed multi-discipline engineering teams across process, mechanical, piping, electrical, and instrumentation. You know how to set a quality bar and hold it

 - Deep fluency in FEL stage-gate processes, PDRI, AACE cost estimate classes, design basis development, and the full document ecosystem that feeds a gate review

 - Fluent across the codes and frameworks that govern these projects (ASME, API, IEEE, NFPA, and the relevant power and interconnection standards), at the level of someone who directs the work, not necessarily runs every calc

 - Strong opinions on where engineering hours get waste

source_jobs.title: VP of Projects
source_jobs.description:
About Candid

Candid is the AI execution layer for capital projects in behind-the-meter gas-fired power generation. We compress pre-FEED and FEED schedules by 30%+ at full engineering quality, run by senior PEs on top of a software platform built around how this work actually gets done. We recently closed a $6.5M round led by Schneider Electric and Yann LeCun. Active pipeline: 200 MW to 2.4 GW BTM gas projects serving AI factories, hyperscale data centers, and gas-to-nuclear bridge architectures across Texas and the Gulf Coast.



The Role

You lead Candid's engineering practice. Walk into the room with a 30-year nuclear-trained CEO or a Fortune 500 head of engineering and be a peer on day one. Hire the project directors. Stamp the deliverables. Defend Candid against incumbent EPCs (B&V, Bechtel, S&L, B&McD, Worley) on FEED bids. Shape what the platform team builds by feeding back what the work actually needs.



What You'll Do

 - Lead engineering practice across BTM gas-fired power projects, 200 MW to multi-GW

 - Serve as senior engineering counterparty to client CEOs, Heads of Engineering, VPs of Projects

 - Stamp FEL-2 / FEED deliverables; defend Class 4 estimates with lenders, IC, incumbent EPCs

 - Build and lead the engineering team — project directors, senior PEs, multi-discipline leads

 - Architect Candid's dual-team execution model (Project Execution Team + FlowState platform team)

 - Shape the platform roadmap with the AI engineering team



What You Bring

 - 20+ years in power generation engineering, deep on combined-cycle and simple-cycle gas

 - Directed engineering on at least two gas plants at FEED or EPC stage ($200M+ capex). Owned, not participated.

 - PE-licensed in Texas (multi-state ideal)

 - Background at a major power EPC (S&L, B&McD, Bechtel Power, B&V, Worley, Kiewit) or owner-operator (Vistra, Constellation, NRG, Talen, Duke, AES)

 - Have run 10+ disciplines in parallel through HAZID, IDC, IFR, IFI cycles

 - BTM / islanded / co-located generation experience — paralleling, redundancy mapping, microgrid architecture

 - Tech-friendly without being naive. You see waste in conventional FEL execution and want to fix it.

 - Houston-based or relocatable



Nice to Have

 - Nuclear-adjacent BOP work (Vogtle, Palisades, NuScale, BWRX-300, Stone & Webster)

 - Data center critical-power experience (Tier III/IV)

 - ERCOT or PJM market familiarity

 - Direct experience with project-finance / IC / lender engineering review

 - Compensation

 - Senior base + meaningful equity. Unlimited PTO. Medical, dental, vision. Daily lunch and dinner. $150/month wellness budget.

source_jobs.description:
What We’re Building

Candid is building the AI layer for EPCs.

We use AI to reduce the manual effort behind preconstruction engineering by automating document understanding, consistency checks, and repetitive workflow steps across major disciplines. Our goal is to make preconstruction 10x faster without sacrificing engineering quality.

Think combined cycle, simple cycle and peakers, gas-to-power for data centers and behind-the-meter campuses, LNG, and cogeneration, with the rotating equipment and balance of plant that goes with them. If your career has been refining or petrochem static equipment with no power work, this isn't the right fit.

This is a hands-on role. Three jobs in one, and the mix shifts as the product gets better.

This is hands-on engineering plus product. Day to day you'll:

 - Do real mechanical engineering on our FEL-2 and early-phase delivery work, owning the parts our software can't handle yet so projects ship

 - Be the quality gate on Candid's automated mechanical outputs: catch what's wrong before it reaches a client, and feed it back so the product gets sharper

 - Define how equipment scope gets developed and validated through FEED on power and gas-to-power projects, and what "good" looks like

 - Build repeatable checks that catch scope gaps and inconsistencies early

 - Map where mechanical rework happens, why it happens, and what inputs drive it

 - Separate the steps that are genuinely repetitive from the ones that need real engineering judgment

You'll help define the checks, workflows, and product requirements that let software take on the repetitive parts, with engineers in control.



What We're Looking For

 - 7+ years at a large scale EPC, PMC, owner-operator, or OEM, with most of it on power generation or gas-to-power

 - Direct experience on combined cycle, simple cycle, gas-to-power, LNG, or cogeneration projects (not refining or petrochem only)

 - You've personally owned mechanical equipment datasheet development and vendor package review through FEED

 - Strong on rotating and power-plant equipment: gas turbines, steam turbines, HRSGs, pumps, compressors, fans, and balance of plant mechanical (cooling systems, fuel gas conditioning, water treatment)

 - Hands-on with equipment specification to API and ASME (API 610, 611, 612, 617; ASME VIII; B31.1 power piping)

 - Experience coordinating OEM turbine and major equipment packages (GE Vernova, Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi Power, or similar)

 - You spot inconsistencies across datasheets, P&IDs, and vendor packages fast

 - Clear understanding of cross-discipline dependencies

 - You've felt boxed in by big-company process and you want out

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and wearing multiple hats; bias to action over waiting for perfect



Nice to Have

 - Discipline lead or technical authority experience

 - Packaged or modular systems and skid integration

 - Real interest in AI and automation



Who Thrives Here

This is a hands-on role.  - Be the quality gate on Candid's automated outputs: catch what's wrong before it reaches a client, and feed it back so the product gets sharper

 - Define how preconstruction process work actually gets done on power and gas-to-power projects, and what "good" looks like

 - Review and validate process deliverables across early-phase packages: heat balances, fuel gas systems, balance of plant, utilities

 - Build repeatable checks that catch inconsistencies across key documents and handoffs

 - Map where rework happens, why it happens, and what inputs drive it

 - Clarify interface points with Mechanical, Piping, Electrical, and Instrumentation

 - Separate the steps that are genuinely repetitive from the ones that need real engineering judgment

You'll help define the checks, workflows, and product requirements that let software take on the repetitive parts, with engineers in control.



What We're Looking For

 - 7+ years at a large scale EPC, PMC, or owner-operator, with most of it on power generation or gas-to-power

 - Direct experience on combined cycle, simple cycle, gas-to-power, LNG, or cogeneration projects (not refining or petrochem only)

 - You've personally owned PFD and P&ID development through FEED

 - Strong on fuel gas systems, balance of plant, and utility systems

 - Hands-on with process simulation: Aspen HYSYS, Aspen Plus, Thermoflow / GT PRO, GateCycle, or similar

 - Solid fundamentals in relief, hydraulics, and equipment sizing

 - Working knowledge of API, ASME, NFPA, ISA, and relevant power codes

 - You spot inconsistencies across P&IDs, datasheets, and vendor packages fast

 - Clear understanding of cross-discipline dependencies

 - You've felt boxed in by big-company process and you want out

Nice to Have

Who Thrives Here

You've outgrown the big-firm machine. You're tired of waiting three weeks for a markup to clear four layers of review, and tired of watching good work die in a stage gate. You want to own your scope end to end, see what you build actually ship, and have a direct line to the founders and the product instead of a 12-person approval chain.

At Candid you'll wear several hats and there's no playbook handing you a defined lane. If

notes: Strong engineering focus, AI-driven preconstruction engineering for EPCs
